applies_when: Require a captured-run manifest when extending eval impact from report generation toward automated benchmark execution; not for report-only metric summaries or immediate agent auto-runner implementation
routing_hint: Route before designing or implementing `eval impact run` or other automated benchmark runners so the run identity, prompt hashes, model settings, variant mapping, confound status, and artifact paths are made auditable first.

applies_when: Design impact-eval task prompts when comparing AI-wiki memory variants; not for running eval workspaces or capturing results
routing_hint: Route when drafting or revising impact eval prompt families and deciding what concrete task should test memory effects.

applies_when: Use trial/error usefulness reporting when extending memory diagnostics to assess whether AI wiki reduced retries, wrong paths, plan changes, or rework cost; not for launching new benchmark families before repeated task evidence exists
routing_hint: Route product/eval clarification tasks here when they ask whether trial-and-error reduction should be measured through aiwiki-toolkit diagnose memory, reuse events, task checks, missed-memory incidents, high-ROI memory reports, or source incident evidence.

applies_when: Check eval-product family scope when a run request expects new benchmark families or names a product theme such as trial-and-error; not for ordinary report smoke over existing captured artifacts.
routing_hint: Route on eval-as-product/impact-eval requests where the user asks whether newer families exist or whether a product-theme family should be designed or run.

applies_when: Design impact-eval comparison modes when deciding whether the primary result should measure end-to-end workflow impact versus raw/consolidated diagnostic variants; not for single-memory causality checks or generic harness implementation.
routing_hint: Route when planning AI Wiki impact evals, especially when comparing workflow-primary outcomes against linked raw/consolidated memory modes.

applies_when: Evaluate AI wiki efficiency when comparing replay outcomes against the original source incident or knowledge acquisition cost; not for routine reuse telemetry or document-level metrics alone
routing_hint: Use for AI wiki evaluation/reporting tasks about time-to-solve, avoided rework, break-even value, or adding source-incident cost alongside no_aiwiki and ambient_aiwiki replay comparisons.

applies_when: Fix impact-eval artifact capture when saved run results must reflect untracked new files as well as tracked diffs; not for eval prompt design, report summarization, or general git workflow docs
routing_hint: Use for manual impact-eval result capture scripts or checks where git status shows new untracked files but result.json, diff patches, or diff stats may omit them.

applies_when: Route source-incident timing provenance when estimating how long original trial/error diagnosis and resolution took; not for generic reuse metrics or candidate discovery without timing evidence.
routing_hint: Use for impact-eval or diagnostics work that needs measured/provenanced source incident duration, especially Codex session or manual timing fields.

applies_when: Tighten manual impact-eval capture when setting up or documenting benchmark rounds; not for ordinary feature tests or generic eval result summaries
routing_hint: Use for manual AI wiki impact eval workflow design, especially when a run depends on visible session/transcript exports and a first-pass analysis cutoff to keep later review from contaminating judgments.

applies_when: Add impact-eval report quality metrics when neutral success rates hide change/churn profile; not for new benchmark-family design.
routing_hint: Use for impact-eval product/reporting work where first-attempt success or score is neutral and the useful signal may be project footprint, managed telemetry, user-owned wiki churn, or ambient-minus-control deltas.

applies_when: Scope eval product MVP when packaging impact or replay evals as a user-run first-attempt report over existing captured artifacts; not for automatic agent-running workflows, final repair analysis, or generic eval scoring.
routing_hint: Use for eval impact report CLI/product-surface decisions: summarize existing run directories, compare primary variants on first_pass metrics, keep final captures diagnostic, and avoid auto-running agents in the MVP.

applies_when: Guide memory consolidation design when planning repo-native AI wiki workflows that turn drafts, trails, or metrics into durable guidance; not for routine end-of-task capture or unrelated shared-doc edits
routing_hint: Use for ai-wiki-toolkit memory lifecycle, consolidation, promotion, freshness, contradiction detection, or Karpathy/Anthropic memory comparison tasks where shared Markdown churn and gated promotion are central concerns.

applies_when: Evaluate route usefulness when comparing route-selected memories against actual reuse, misses, and noisy selections; not for ordinary route invocation or general wiki reuse dashboards
routing_hint: Route to this when designing route-quality metrics, route trace logging, or selected-vs-used memory comparisons.

applies_when: Recover local Codex sessions when the user asks to find a missing Codex chat/window from a remembered prompt fragment; not for ChatGPT app history recovery, temporary export directories, or unrelated AI Wiki workflow checks
routing_hint: Use for local Codex session recovery: search ~/.codex/sessions JSONL with a distinctive prompt fragment, then map the session id through ~/.codex/session_index.jsonl or ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite threads metadata; mention codex resume when reopening is requested.
